svg.text: MISHNAIC HEBREW
HISTORY — RABBINIC AGE AND ISLAM
THE MISHNAIC PERIOD
70 CE: Temple destroyed; Judaism transforms to rabbinic model
Language of prayer and law: Hebrew • Language of daily life: Aramaic
Mishnah (~200 CE): Rabbi Yehuda HaNasi codifies oral law in Hebrew
Jerusalem Talmud (~400 CE) • Babylonian Talmud (~500 CE): Aramaic base
JEWISH LIFE UNDER ROME AND PERSIA
Land of Israel: Roman then Byzantine rule; Hebrew persists in study
Babylonian Jews: under Sassanid Persian rule; Aramaic dominant
Geonim (589-1038 CE): heads of Babylonian academies; Hebrew responsa
RISE OF ISLAM AND ARABIC
622 CE: Hijra — Muhammad flees Mecca to Medina; Islam spreads rapidly
638 CE: Arab armies take Jerusalem from Byzantines
Jews of Levant transition from Aramaic to Arabic as daily language
Hebrew remains sacred; Arabic becomes new vernacular for most Jews
ARABIC HISTORY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
THE GOLDEN AGE OF JEWISH-ARABIC CULTURE
~800-1200 CE: Islamic civilization peaks; Jews full participants
Sa'adia Gaon (882-942): first major Jewish philosopher, wrote in Arabic
Maimonides (1138-1204): Mishneh Torah (Hebrew) + Guide (Judeo-Arabic)
Hebrew poetry: Andalusian Jews adapted Arabic meter and forms
JUDEO-ARABIC: THE BRIDGE
Judeo-Arabic: Arabic language written in Hebrew characters
Used by Jewish communities across Islamic world for 1000 years
Cairo Geniza (discovered 1896): ~300,000 Judeo-Arabic documents
TWO FAITHS, TWO LANGUAGES, ONE CULTURE
Convivencia in Andalusia (~900-1200): Jews, Muslims, Christians coexist
Arabic-Hebrew bilingualism: normal among educated Jews for 400+ years
Hebrew grammar books written in Arabic (Ibn Janah, Ibn Ezra)
Mishnaic Hebrew preserved in study; Arabic shaped Jewish civilization
PLATE 10d ALT · MISHNAIC HEBREW / ARABIC · HISTORY
